question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NftAllowanceRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findAll(NftAllowanceRequest, EntityId)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getBaseCondition() method's unconditional AND with APPROVAL_CONDITION to determine that only approvedForAll=true allowances are returned, and use this as a side-channel to infer whether a specific NFT allowance is approvedForAll or not by observing whether it appears in query results?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NftAllowanceRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findAll(NftAllowanceRequest, EntityId)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the isOwner=false path in getBaseCondition() to query by spender instead of owner, enumerating all NFT token IDs for which a target account has been granted spender rights, leaking the target account's received allowances without any authorization?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NftAllowanceRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findAll(NftAllowanceRequest, EntityId)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the isOwner=true path in getBaseCondition() to query by owner, enumerating all accounts to which a target account has granted NFT allowances, leaking the target account's granted allowances and revealing its token portfolio and delegation relationships?
